title: Theory of Computation | Fill in the Blanks Quiz
heading: Theory of Computation (ToC) - Fill in the Blanks
description: Test your knowledge of Theory of Computation with these fill-in-the-blank questions.

questions.question: A sequence of symbols taken from an alphabet is called a _____________.
questions.hint: It is a finite sequence of symbols.

questions.question: A _____________ shows the hierarchical structure of a string according to a context-free grammar.
questions.hint: Also known as a derivation tree.

questions.question: A formal language is defined as a set of _____________ over a given alphabet.
questions.hint: These are formed by concatenating symbols from the alphabet.

questions.question: In a context-free grammar, a _____________ is a symbol that can be replaced by strings of terminals and/or non-terminals.
questions.hint: These are usually represented by uppercase letters.

questions.question: A finite automaton that has exactly one transition for each symbol is called a _____________.
questions.hint: Deterministic Finite Automaton.

questions.question: A finite automaton that can have multiple transitions for the same input is called a _____________.
questions.hint: Non-deterministic Finite Automaton (also NDFA).

questions.question: The operation used to represent zero or more repetitions of a symbol is called _____________.
questions.hint: Represented by the * symbol.

questions.question: The theorem that establishes equivalence between regular expressions and finite automata is called _____________.
questions.hint: Named after the same mathematician as the star operation.

questions.question: The process of reducing the number of states in a DFA is called _____________.
questions.hint: It involves merging equivalent states.

questions.question: A grammar is said to be _____________ if a string has more than one parse tree.
questions.hint: This is often undesirable for programming languages.

questions.question: The Pumping Lemma for regular languages is mainly used to prove that a language is _____________.
questions.hint: It is a proof by contradiction technique.

questions.question: The Pumping Lemma for context-free languages is used to show that a language is not _____________.
questions.hint: It is more complex than the version for regular languages.

questions.question: The set of all possible strings over an alphabet is denoted by _____________.
questions.hint: Sigma Star (includes the empty string).

questions.question: Symbols that appear in the final strings of a language are called _____________.
questions.hint: These cannot be replaced further in a grammar.

questions.question: A _____________ is a 5-tuple used to define a finite automaton.
questions.hint: Its components include states, alphabet, transitions, start state, and final states.

questions.question: The closure property that allows combining two languages is called _____________.
questions.hint: Other properties include concatenation and Kleene star.

questions.question: Removing useless and unreachable symbols is part of _____________ of a CFG.
questions.hint: It helps in converting the grammar to normal forms.

questions.question: A tree representing derivation of a string is called a _____________.
questions.hint: Also known as a parse tree.

questions.question: A grammar in which every production is of the form A → BC or A → a is called _____________.
questions.hint: Abbreviated as CNF.

questions.question: The automaton that uses a stack as memory is called a _____________.
questions.hint: Abbreviated as PDA.
